records.ety: [L. implicatus, p. p. of implicare to involve; pref. im- in + plicare to fold. See Employ, Ply, and cf. Imply, Implicit.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Implicated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Implicating.]

defs: To infold; to fold together; to interweave.
quotes: The meeting boughs and implicated leaves.

defs: To bring into connection with; to involve; to connect; -- applied to persons, in an unfavorable sense; as, the evidence implicates many in this conspiracy; to be implicated in a crime, a discreditable transaction, a fault, etc.
